Science & Technology

176 solved questions in this topic · SSC GD General Knowledge & General Awareness

Home
Q.No 25 Science & TechnologyEnglish
When is acceleration considered positive?
A
When it is opposite to velocity
B
When velocity is decreasing
C
When it is in the direction of velocity
✓ Correct
D
When the object is at rest

Solution

Correct answer: C. Acceleration is taken as positive when it acts in the same direction as the velocity, because the body then speeds up in its direction of motion.

A is incorrect: When acceleration is opposite to velocity it is negative acceleration (retardation/deceleration).

B is incorrect: Decreasing velocity means the acceleration opposes motion, i.e. it is negative.

D is incorrect: An object at rest with no change in velocity has zero acceleration.

💡 Acceleration is a vector quantity, so its sign depends on the chosen positive direction relative to the velocity.
Q.No 29 Science & TechnologyEnglish
if a spacecraft travels at 30 km/s, how long will it take to cover 1 AU (150 million km)?
A
15 days
B
58 days
✓ Correct
C
100 days
D
365 days

Solution

Correct answer: B. Time = distance / speed = 150,000,000 km / 30 km/s = 5,000,000 seconds, which is about 57.9 days, i.e. roughly 58 days.

A is incorrect: 15 days would require a speed of about 116 km/s.

C is incorrect: 100 days corresponds to roughly 17 km/s.

D is incorrect: 365 days (one year) corresponds to a speed of under 5 km/s.

💡 1 AU is the average Earth-Sun distance; light covers it in about 8 minutes 20 seconds.
Q.No 24 Science & TechnologyHindi
चंद्रमा पर पृथ्वी की तुलना में कम गुरुत्वाकर्षण बल क्यों लगता है?
A
यह आकार में छोटा है
B
इसका द्रव्यमान कम है
✓ Correct
C
यह तेजी से घूमता है
D
यह सूर्य से अधिक दूर है

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Moon exerts less gravitational pull than the Earth mainly because it has much less mass (about 1/81 of Earth's mass); gravity depends on mass, so surface gravity on the Moon is about one-sixth of Earth's.

A is incorrect: Smaller size alone does not determine gravity; it is the mass that matters, though the Moon is indeed smaller.

C is incorrect: The Moon actually rotates slowly (tidally locked), and rotation speed does not reduce gravity.

D is incorrect: Distance from the Sun is irrelevant to the Moon's own surface gravity.

💡 Surface gravity g = GM/R^2; the Moon's lower mass M is the dominant reason its g is about 1.62 m/s^2.
Q.No 27 Science & TechnologyHindi
पृथ्वी का चुंबकीय क्षेत्र हमें आवेशित सौर कणों से संरक्षित करता है। जब पृथ्वी का चुंबकीय क्षेत्र अस्थायी रूप से दुर्बल हो जाता है, तो क्या होता है?
A
ध्रुवीय ज्योति और विकिरण जोखिम में वृद्धि होती है
✓ Correct
B
वायुमंडल की पूर्ण हानि होती है
C
गुरुत्वाकर्षण में वृद्धि होती है
D
कोई प्रभाव नहीं होता है

Solution

Correct answer: A. Earth's magnetic field deflects charged solar particles. When it weakens temporarily, more particles reach the upper atmosphere, increasing auroral activity (polar lights) and the radiation exposure for satellites and astronauts.

B is incorrect: A temporary weakening does not strip away the entire atmosphere.

C is incorrect: The magnetic field has nothing to do with the strength of gravity.

D is incorrect: There is a clear effect - increased auroras and radiation - so 'no effect' is wrong.

💡 The region of space dominated by Earth's magnetic field is called the magnetosphere.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
यौवनारंभ के दौरान पुरुषों में होने वाले परिवर्तनों के लिए मुख्य रूप से कौन-सा हार्मोन उत्तरदायी है?
A
एस्ट्रोजन
B
थायरोक्सिन
C
टेस्टोस्टेरोन
✓ Correct
D
एड्रिनिेलिन

Solution

Correct answer: C. Testosterone is the primary male sex hormone responsible for the physical changes of puberty in boys, such as deepening of the voice, facial hair and muscle growth.

A is incorrect: Estrogen is the primary female sex hormone driving female pubertal changes.

B is incorrect: Thyroxine, from the thyroid gland, regulates metabolism, not male puberty specifically.

D is incorrect: Adrenaline is the 'fight-or-flight' hormone from the adrenal medulla, not a puberty hormone.

💡 Testosterone is produced mainly in the testes; the onset of puberty is triggered by the hypothalamus releasing GnRH.
Q.No 37 Science & TechnologyHindi
आयुर्वेद के जनक के रूप में किसे जाना जाता है?
A
Charak
✓ Correct
B
Vagbhata
C
Sushruta
D
Nagarjuna

Solution

Correct answer: A. Charak (Charaka) is regarded as the father of Ayurveda (Indian medicine); his treatise, the Charaka Samhita, is the foundational text of Ayurvedic internal medicine.

B is incorrect: Vagbhata was a later Ayurvedic scholar who compiled the Ashtanga Hridayam.

C is incorrect: Sushruta is honoured as the father of surgery (Sushruta Samhita), not of Ayurveda as a whole.

D is incorrect: Nagarjuna was a scholar associated with alchemy/rasashastra, not the founding of Ayurveda.

💡 Charaka served in the court of the Kushan emperor Kanishka and emphasised prevention and a balanced lifestyle.
Q.No 24 Science & TechnologyHindi
यदि दो वस्तुओं के बीच की दूरी छह गुना बढ़ जाए तो गुरुत्वाकर्षण बल पर क्या प्रभाव पड़ेगा?
A
36 गुना बड़ा हो जाता है
B
6 गुना बड़ा हो जाता है
C
36 गुना छोटा हो जाता है
✓ Correct
D
/6 गुना छोटा हो जाता है

Solution

Correct answer: C. By Newton's law of gravitation, force is inversely proportional to the square of the distance. If the distance increases 6 times, the force becomes 1/6^2 = 1/36, i.e. 36 times smaller.

A is incorrect: Force decreases, not increases, when distance grows, so '36 times larger' is wrong.

B is incorrect: Force does not increase 6 times; it decreases with the square of distance.

D is incorrect: Force falls by the square (1/36), not just 1/6.

💡 Newton's law: F = G m1 m2 / r^2, the inverse-square law of gravitation.
Q.No 30 Science & TechnologyEnglish
एक इंजीनियर को रडार का इस्तेमाल करके धरती की सतह में होने वाले बदलावों पर नज़र रखने का काम सौंपा गया है, यहाँ तक कि बादलों के बीच और रात में भी।
A
Chandrayaan-3
B
EOS-09
C
NISAR
✓ Correct
D
XPoSat

Solution

Correct answer: C. NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) is a joint Earth-observation satellite that uses radar to monitor changes in the Earth's surface in all weather, day or night, even through clouds.

A is incorrect: Chandrayaan-3 is India's lunar lander-rover mission, not an Earth-surface radar satellite.

B is incorrect: EOS-09 is an Earth-observation satellite but is not the NASA-ISRO radar collaboration described.

D is incorrect: XPoSat is an X-ray polarimetry satellite for studying cosmic sources, not Earth's surface.

💡 NISAR carries dual-frequency (L- and S-band) radar, the first satellite of its kind, to track land deformation, ice and ecosystems.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyEnglish
किस भारतीय रॉकेट को "फैट बॉय" कहा जाता है?
A
PSLV
B
GSLV MK III
✓ Correct
C
SSLV
D
ASLV

Solution

Correct answer: B. The GSLV Mk III (now called LVM3) is nicknamed the 'Fat Boy' because of its heavy, bulky build; it is ISRO's most powerful launch vehicle.

A is incorrect: The PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's reliable workhorse, not the 'Fat Boy'.

C is incorrect: The SSLV is the Small Satellite Launch Vehicle, a lightweight rocket.

D is incorrect: The ASLV was an early, much smaller experimental launch vehicle.

💡 LVM3 launched Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3 and will carry India's Gaganyaan crewed mission.
Q.No 29 Science & TechnologyHindi
4,000 वर्ष (मिलेनकोविच चक्र) में पृथ्वी के अक्ष का झुकाव 22.* से 24.5" के बीच बदलता रहता है। यह परिवर्तन पृथ्वी को किस प्रकार प्रभावित करता है?
A
अधिक झुकाव से मौसमी विपर्यास में वृद्धि होती है
✓ Correct
B
झुकाव परिवर्तन से कक्षीय चाल बदलता है
C
झुकाव परिवर्तन चुंबकीय क्षेत्र को प्रभावित करता है
D
कोई प्रभाव नहीं होता है

Solution

Correct answer: A. Over the roughly 41,000-year Milankovitch cycle, Earth's axial tilt (obliquity) varies between about 22.1 and 24.5 degrees; a greater tilt increases the contrast between seasons (hotter summers, colder winters).

B is incorrect: A change in axial tilt does not change the Earth's orbital speed.

C is incorrect: Obliquity affects climate/seasons, not the planet's magnetic field.

D is incorrect: There is a clear climatic effect, so 'no effect' is wrong.

💡 Milankovitch cycles - changes in eccentricity, obliquity and precession - drive long-term ice-age cycles.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyHindi
ओरायन (000), एक है।
A
Constellation
✓ Correct
B
Star
C
Galaxy
D
Satellite

Solution

Correct answer: A. Orion is a constellation - a recognisable pattern of stars in the night sky, prominent in winter and known for the three stars of 'Orion's Belt'.

B is incorrect: Orion is not a single star; it contains many stars such as Betelgeuse and Rigel.

C is incorrect: Orion is not a galaxy; it is a group of stars within our own Milky Way galaxy.

D is incorrect: Orion is not a satellite.

💡 The Orion Nebula, a stellar nursery, lies in this constellation and is visible to the naked eye.
Q.No 34 Science & TechnologyHindi
हृदय स्पंद, किस प्रकार की क्रिया है?
A
प्रतिवर्ती क्रिया
B
अनैच्छिक क्रिया
✓ Correct
C
ऐच्छिक क्रिया
D
समन्वित क्रिया

Solution

Correct answer: B. The heartbeat is an involuntary action - it occurs automatically and continuously without conscious control, driven by the heart's own pacemaker.

A is incorrect: A reflex action is a sudden, automatic response to a stimulus (e.g. a knee-jerk), not the continuous heartbeat.

C is incorrect: A voluntary action is one we consciously control, such as walking.

D is incorrect: 'Coordinated action' is not the standard classification for the heartbeat.

💡 The heartbeat is initiated by the sino-atrial (SA) node, the heart's natural pacemaker, and regulated by the autonomic nervous system.
Q.No 35 Science & TechnologyHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा, कोशिका में लाइसोसोम का प्राथमिक कार्य है?
A
ऊर्जा उत्पादन
B
अपशिष्ट निपटान
✓ Correct
C
प्रोटीन संश्लेषण
D
लिपिड का भंडारण

Solution

Correct answer: B. The primary function of the lysosome is intracellular waste disposal - it contains digestive enzymes that break down worn-out cell parts, food particles and foreign material.

A is incorrect: Energy production is the function of the mitochondria.

C is incorrect: Protein synthesis is carried out by ribosomes.

D is incorrect: Lipid storage is not the lysosome's primary role.

💡 Because they digest cellular debris and can break down the cell itself, lysosomes are nicknamed the cell's 'suicide bags'.
Q.No 24 Science & TechnologyEnglish
Archimedes’ principle states that a body immersed in a fluid experiences an upward force equal to
A
Its own weight
B
The volume of the body
C
The weight of the fluid displaced
✓ Correct
D
The density of the fluid

Solution

Correct answer: C. Archimedes' principle states that a body immersed (fully or partly) in a fluid experiences an upward buoyant force equal to the weight of the fluid it displaces.

A is incorrect: The buoyant force equals the displaced fluid's weight, not the body's own weight (these are equal only when the body floats).

B is incorrect: The volume of the body is a quantity, not a force.

D is incorrect: Density of the fluid alone is not a force; the upthrust depends on weight of fluid displaced.

💡 This principle explains why ships float and why objects feel lighter in water.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyEnglish
Which telescope provided the first direct image of a black hole?
A
Hubble
B
Event Horizon Telescope
✓ Correct
C
Spitzer
D
Kepler

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Event Horizon Telescope (EHT) produced the first-ever direct image of a black hole - the supermassive black hole at the centre of galaxy M87 - released in April 2019.

A is incorrect: The Hubble Space Telescope is an optical/UV observatory and did not capture the first black-hole image.

C is incorrect: The Spitzer Space Telescope was an infrared observatory, not the source of this image.

D is incorrect: The Kepler telescope was designed to detect exoplanets, not image black holes.

💡 The EHT is a global network of radio telescopes that works together as an Earth-sized virtual telescope.
Q.No 32 Science & TechnologyEnglish⚠ low OCR confidence
if an astronaut’s mass is 70 kg, what will be their weight on the Moon (1/6th of Earth's gravity)?
A
700 N
B
117 N
✓ Correct
C
420 N
D
686 N

Solution

Correct answer: B. Weight on the Moon = mass x lunar gravity = 70 kg x (9.8/6) m/s^2 ≈ 70 x 1.63 ≈ 114-117 N.

A is incorrect: 700 N (using g = 10) would be the approximate weight on Earth, not the Moon.

C is incorrect: 420 N would imply gravity far higher than one-sixth of Earth's.

D is incorrect: 686 N (70 x 9.8) is the weight on Earth, not on the Moon.

💡 Mass stays constant everywhere; only weight (= mass x local gravity) changes between the Earth and the Moon.
Q.No 30 Science & TechnologyHindi
यूरेनस का अक्षीय झुकाव (8:09 (॥) लगभग 98" है, यानी यह लगभग अपने पार्श्र (30६6) पर घूर्णन करता है। इसका यूरेनस के मौसमों पर क्या प्रभाव पड़ता है?
A
अत्यधिक मौसमी परिवर्तन
✓ Correct
B
कोई मौसमी परिवर्तन नहीं होता है
C
मृदु मौसम
D
एक पार्श्श पर स्थायी दिन

Solution

Correct answer: A. Uranus has an axial tilt of about 98 degrees, so it rotates almost on its side; this produces extreme seasonal variation, with each pole facing the Sun continuously for about 42 years.

B is incorrect: With such an extreme tilt there are very strong seasonal changes, not none.

C is incorrect: The seasons are extreme, not mild.

D is incorrect: While a pole does get long days, the overall effect is extreme seasonal change, which option A captures best.

💡 Uranus's extreme tilt is thought to have resulted from a massive collision early in the solar system's history.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyHindi
00 प्रकाश वर्ष दूर स्थित किसी तारे से प्रकाश को पृथ्वी तक पहुँचने में लगभग 00 वर्ष लगते हैं। विस्फोट के बाद (यदि वह अभी विस्फोट करता है) तारा हमें कितने समय तक दिखाई देगा?
A
Immediately
B
After 100 years
✓ Correct
C
After 1 year
D
Never

Solution

Correct answer: B. Light from a star 100 light-years away takes 100 years to reach Earth, so if the star explodes now, we would see the explosion only after 100 years.

A is incorrect: Light is not instantaneous; it travels at a finite speed.

C is incorrect: One year is far too short for light to cross 100 light-years.

D is incorrect: We will eventually see it - after the 100-year light-travel time.

💡 Because of this delay, when we look at distant stars we are effectively seeing them as they were in the past.
Q.No 34 Science & TechnologyHindi
अस्थि, प्राणि-ऊतकों की किस श्रेणी से संबंधित है?
A
पेशीय ऊतक
B
संयोजी ऊतक
✓ Correct
C
तंत्रिका ऊतक
D
उपकला ऊतक

Solution

Correct answer: B. Bone is a type of connective tissue - it connects and supports other tissues and has a hard matrix of collagen and calcium salts.

A is incorrect: Muscular tissue is made of contractile fibres for movement.

C is incorrect: Nervous tissue transmits electrical impulses.

D is incorrect: Epithelial tissue forms protective linings and coverings.

💡 Other connective tissues include blood, cartilage and adipose (fat) tissue.
Q.No 35 Science & TechnologyHindi
हरितलवक का प्राथमिक कार्य क्या है?
A
स्टार्च का भंडारण
B
प्रोटीन संश्लेषण
C
माइटोकॉन्ड्रिया जैसा ऊर्जा उत्पादन
D
प्रकाश संश्लेषण
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. The primary function of the chloroplast is photosynthesis - it contains chlorophyll, which captures sunlight to make food (glucose) from carbon dioxide and water.

A is incorrect: Starch storage is a secondary role; the chloroplast's main job is photosynthesis.

B is incorrect: Protein synthesis is carried out by ribosomes.

C is incorrect: Energy production by respiration is the work of mitochondria, not chloroplasts.

💡 Chloroplasts are found only in plant cells and some algae, giving plants their green colour.
Q.No 24 Science & TechnologyEnglish⚠ low OCR confidence
प्रतिध्वनि को स्पष्ट रूप से सुनने के लिए ध्वनि के स्रोत से बाधा की न्यूनतम दूरी होती है
A
34.4 m
B
22.5 m
C
17.2 m
✓ Correct
D
10.6 m

Solution

Correct answer: C. To hear a distinct echo, the reflected sound must return after at least 0.1 s; with sound speed about 344 m/s, the obstacle must be at least (344 x 0.1)/2 = 17.2 m away.

A is incorrect: 34.4 m is the total path (to and back), not the one-way minimum distance.

B is incorrect: 22.5 m does not correspond to the 0.1-second persistence-of-hearing limit.

D is incorrect: 10.6 m is too short; the echo would merge with the original sound.

💡 The 0.1-second limit comes from the persistence of sound in human ears.
Q.No 30 Science & TechnologyHindi
इसरो (।5९0) द्वारा 2025 में अनावरण किये जाने वाले भारतीय अंतरिक्ष स्टेशन (8/5) का नियोजित उद्देश्य कया है?
A
मंगल ग्रह अन्वेषण
B
चंद्र मानचित्रण
C
अंतरिक्ष पर्यटन और स्टेशन समर्थन
✓ Correct
D
गहरे अंतरिक्ष संचार

Solution

Correct answer: C. Per the question's framing, the planned Bharatiya Antariksha Station is intended to support a human presence in orbit - sustained station operations and related activities - rather than planetary or lunar missions.

A is incorrect: Mars exploration is a separate ISRO objective, not the purpose of the space station.

B is incorrect: Lunar mapping is handled by the Chandrayaan missions.

D is incorrect: Deep-space communication is supported by ground networks, not the station's purpose.

💡 ISRO plans to establish the Bharatiya Antariksha Station (BAS) in stages following the Gaganyaan human-spaceflight programme.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyHindi
फरवरी 2025 में इसरो की इंजन सुविधाओं में किसका सफलतापूर्वक परीक्षण किया गया?
A
क्रायोजेनिक इंजन कक्षा में पुन: आरंभ
B
निर्वात में (६20 इंजन प्रज्वलन
✓ Correct
C
मानव-रेटेड जीवन समर्थन
D
सौर पाल की तैनाती

Solution

Correct answer: B. In February 2025, ISRO successfully demonstrated ignition of the CE20 cryogenic engine in vacuum conditions at its engine-test facilities, an important capability for upper-stage performance.

A is incorrect: The test was a vacuum ignition demonstration, not an in-orbit cryogenic restart.

C is incorrect: It was an engine test, not a human-rated life-support test.

D is incorrect: Solar-sail deployment is unrelated to this engine test.

💡 The CE20 is the indigenous cryogenic engine that powers the upper stage of India's LVM3 rocket.
Q.No 29 Science & TechnologyBengali
যদি নক্ষত্রটি সূর্যের মতো হয়, তবে পৃথিবীর তুলনায় বহির্রহটির পৃষ্ঠের তাপমাত্রা সম্পর্কে কী অনুমান করা যেতে পারে?
A
তাৎপর্যপূর্ণভাবে উষ্ণতর
B
একই তাপমাত্রা
✓ Correct
C
শীতল
D
দূরত্বের কারণে তাপমাত্রায় প্রভাব পড়ে না

Solution

Correct answer: B. If the star is like our Sun and the exoplanet orbits at an Earth-like distance, its surface temperature would be roughly the same as Earth's, since temperature depends mainly on the star's output and the planet's distance.

A is incorrect: It would be significantly hotter only if it orbited much closer to the star.

C is incorrect: It would be colder only if it were much farther from the star.

D is incorrect: Distance does affect temperature, so 'no effect of distance' is wrong.

💡 The zone around a star where temperatures allow liquid water is called the 'habitable' or 'Goldilocks' zone.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyBengali
পৃথিবীর অক্ষের fags অগ্রগমন কী কারণে ঘটে?
A
সূর্য ও টাদের মাধ্যাকর্ষণীয় আকর্ষণ
✓ Correct
B
পৃথিবীর আবর্তনের গতি
C
পৃথিবীর কক্ষপথের ভিন্নতা
D
সৌর বায়ু

Solution

Correct answer: A. The slow precession (wobble) of the Earth's axis is caused mainly by the gravitational pull of the Sun and the Moon on the Earth's equatorial bulge.

B is incorrect: The speed of Earth's rotation does not cause the axial precession.

C is incorrect: Variation in the orbit's shape (eccentricity) is a separate Milankovitch effect.

D is incorrect: The solar wind is far too weak to cause precession.

💡 One full precession cycle of the Earth's axis takes about 26,000 years.
Q.No 34 Science & TechnologyBengali
Ph কোষের প্রাচীরে কোন পদার্থ বা উপাদান থাকে, যা সেগুলিকে গ্যাস ও জলের প্রতি অভেদ্য করে তোলে?
A
কিউটিন
B
লিগনিন
C
সাবেরিন
✓ Correct
D
সেলুলোজ

Solution

Correct answer: C. Suberin is the waxy substance deposited in the cell walls of cork (and the root endodermis), making them impermeable to water and gases.

A is incorrect: Cutin forms the protective cuticle on leaf surfaces, but the cell-wall waterproofing substance asked here is suberin.

B is incorrect: Lignin provides rigidity to woody tissues but does not chiefly make cells gas/water-impermeable.

D is incorrect: Cellulose is the main structural carbohydrate of cell walls but is permeable to water.

💡 Suberin in the Casparian strip of the root controls water and mineral uptake into the plant.
Q.No 35 Science & TechnologyBengali
কতগুলি মেমত্রেন বা পর্দা দ্বারা একটি মাইটোকন্ত্রিয়া (mitochondrion) আবৃত থাকে?
A
একটি
B
দুটি
✓ Correct
C
তিনটি
D
চারটি

Solution

Correct answer: B. A mitochondrion is bounded by two membranes - a smooth outer membrane and a folded inner membrane (whose folds are called cristae).

A is incorrect: It has two membranes, not one.

C is incorrect: Three is incorrect; mitochondria are double-membraned.

D is incorrect: Four membranes is wrong.

💡 The inner-membrane folds (cristae) increase surface area for ATP (energy) production.
Q.No 24 Science & TechnologyEnglish
The rolling of thunder is mainly due to
A
Resonance of clouds
B
Successive reflections of sound
✓ Correct
C
Lightning effect
D
Interference of sound waves

Solution

Correct answer: B. The rolling of thunder is mainly due to the successive reflections of the sound of the lightning discharge from clouds, hills and the ground, which reach the listener at slightly different times.

A is incorrect: Resonance of clouds is not the cause of the prolonged rumble.

C is incorrect: The lightning is the source of the sound, but the 'rolling' is from reflections.

D is incorrect: Interference of waves is not the principal reason for the rumbling sound.

💡 We see lightning before we hear thunder because light travels much faster than sound.
Q.No 30 Science & TechnologyEnglish
What capability does Project NETRA provide to ISRO?
A
Deep-space communication
B
Space debris tracking and situational awareness
✓ Correct
C
Human-rated life support
D
Lunar landing navigation

Solution

Correct answer: B. Project NETRA (Network for Space Object Tracking and Analysis) gives ISRO the capability to track space debris and maintain space situational awareness to protect Indian satellites.

A is incorrect: Deep-space communication is handled by the Deep Space Network, not NETRA.

C is incorrect: Human-rated life support is part of the Gaganyaan programme.

D is incorrect: Lunar-landing navigation is part of the Chandrayaan missions.

💡 Space situational awareness helps avoid collisions between satellites and orbital debris.
Q.No 32 Science & TechnologyEnglish
India wants to explore Venus’s thick atmosphere and greenhouse effect. Which mission is planned?
A
LUPEX
B
Shukrayaan (Venus Orbiter)
✓ Correct
C
Chandrayaan-4
D
MOM-2

Solution

Correct answer: B. India's planned mission to study Venus's thick atmosphere and greenhouse effect is Shukrayaan, a Venus Orbiter Mission.

A is incorrect: LUPEX is the planned Lunar Polar Exploration mission (with Japan).

C is incorrect: Chandrayaan-4 is a lunar sample-return mission, not a Venus mission.

D is incorrect: MOM-2 (Mangalyaan-2) is aimed at Mars, not Venus.

💡 Venus has a runaway greenhouse effect, making it the hottest planet in the solar system despite being farther from the Sun than Mercury.
Q.No 28 Science & TechnologyEnglish
Why are the days on Venus longer than its years?
A
Venus has a slower rotation than revolution
✓ Correct
B
Venus revolves very slowly around the Sun
C
Venus does not rotate
D
Venus spins on its axis in the opposite direction

Solution

Correct answer: A. A day on Venus is longer than its year because Venus rotates on its axis very slowly - its single rotation (day) takes longer than one revolution around the Sun (year).

B is incorrect: Venus actually revolves around the Sun faster than it spins, which is why the year is shorter.

C is incorrect: Venus does rotate, just extremely slowly.

D is incorrect: Venus does spin in the opposite (retrograde) direction, but the day-longer-than-year fact is due to its slow rotation.

💡 One Venus day equals about 243 Earth days, while a Venus year is about 225 Earth days.
Q.No 30 Science & TechnologyEnglish
Which planet has a magnetic field that is offset from its center and tilted about 59 degrees to its rotational axis?
A
Earth
B
Jupiter
C
Uranus
✓ Correct
D
Saturn

Solution

Correct answer: C. Uranus has a magnetic field that is offset from the planet's centre and tilted by about 59 degrees to its rotational axis - highly unusual among the planets.

A is incorrect: Earth's magnetic field is only slightly tilted (about 11 degrees) and roughly centred.

B is incorrect: Jupiter's field is strong but not tilted by ~59 degrees in this offset way.

D is incorrect: Saturn's magnetic axis is almost aligned with its rotation axis.

💡 Uranus's strange tilted, off-centre magnetic field may be generated in its icy mantle rather than a metallic core.
Q.No 33 Science & TechnologyEnglish
Blood is classified under which type of animal tissue?
A
Muscular tissue
B
Nervous tissue
C
Connective tissue
✓ Correct
D
Epithelial tissue

Solution

Correct answer: C. Blood is classified as connective tissue because its cells are suspended in a fluid matrix (plasma) and it connects and transports materials throughout the body.

A is incorrect: Muscular tissue is contractile, unlike blood.

B is incorrect: Nervous tissue conducts impulses; blood does not.

D is incorrect: Epithelial tissue forms linings; blood is a fluid connective tissue.

💡 Plasma is the liquid matrix of blood, carrying red cells, white cells and platelets.
Q.No 34 Science & TechnologyEnglish
Which of the following is an example of chemotropism in plants?
A
Shoot bending towards light
B
Root growing downwards
C
Pollen tube growing towards ovule
✓ Correct
D
Leaf folding on touch

Solution

Correct answer: C. A pollen tube growing towards the ovule (in response to chemical signals from the ovary) is an example of chemotropism - growth directed by a chemical stimulus.

A is incorrect: A shoot bending towards light is phototropism.

B is incorrect: A root growing downwards is geotropism (gravitropism).

D is incorrect: A leaf folding on touch (as in Mimosa) is thigmonasty/seismonasty, not chemotropism.

💡 Tropic movements are directional growth responses to stimuli such as light, gravity, water or chemicals.
Q.No 24 Science & TechnologyHindi
न्यूटन के पहले नियम के अनुसार, कोई वस्तु तब तक स्थिर या एकसमान गति में रहती है जब तक
A
इस पर बैलेंस्ड फोर्स काम करते हैं।
B
गुरुत्वाकर्षण इसकी गति बदलता है।
C
लगाया गया बल बदलाव के लिए मजबूर करता है।
✓ Correct
D
घर्षण दूर हो जाता है।

Solution

Correct answer: C. By Newton's first law (law of inertia), a body stays at rest or in uniform motion unless an applied (net external) force compels it to change its state.

A is incorrect: Under balanced forces the body simply continues its state; it is an unbalanced force that changes motion.

B is incorrect: Gravity is just one possible force; the law is about any net force.

D is incorrect: Removing friction does not by itself cause change; a net applied force does.

💡 Newton's first law defines inertia - the tendency of a body to resist changes in its motion.
Q.No 28 Science & TechnologyHindi
932 में न्यूट्रॉन की खोज का श्रेय किस वैज्ञानिक को दिया जाता है?
A
अर्नेस्ट रदरफोर्ड
B
नील्स बोहर
C
जेम्स चैडविक
✓ Correct
D
वोल्फगैंग पाउली

Solution

Correct answer: C. The neutron was discovered in 1932 by James Chadwick, for which he received the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1935.

A is incorrect: Ernest Rutherford discovered the nucleus and the proton, and predicted the neutron, but did not discover it.

B is incorrect: Niels Bohr gave the model of the atom.

D is incorrect: Wolfgang Pauli proposed the neutrino and the exclusion principle.

💡 The neutron is a neutral subatomic particle found in the nucleus along with protons.
Q.No 30 Science & TechnologyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
किस भारतीय सौर मिशन ने 500 उपकरण का उपयोग करके नियमित विज्ञान अवलोकन शुरू किया और 2025 की शुरुआत में अपना पहला डेटा सेट जारी किया?
A
AstroSat-2
B
Aditya-L1
✓ Correct
C
Chandrayaan-3
D
XPoSat

Solution

Correct answer: B. Aditya-L1, India's first dedicated solar mission, began regular science observations and released its early datasets, studying the Sun from the L1 point.

A is incorrect: AstroSat-2 is not yet an operational solar mission.

C is incorrect: Chandrayaan-3 is a lunar mission, not a solar observatory.

D is incorrect: XPoSat studies cosmic X-ray sources, not the Sun's regular observations described.

💡 Aditya-L1 orbits the Sun-Earth Lagrange point L1, about 1.5 million km from Earth, for an uninterrupted view of the Sun.
Q.No 32 Science & TechnologyHindi
लैंडर की विफलता के बावजूद चंद्रयान-2 मिशन में क्या महत्वपूर्ण था?
A
इसने शुक्र ग्रह का अध्ययन किया
B
इसका ऑर्बिटर डेटा भेजना जारी रखता है
✓ Correct
C
यह अंतरिक्ष यात्रियों को ले गया
D
यह भारत का पहला मंगल मिशन था

Solution

Correct answer: B. Despite the lander's failure, the Chandrayaan-2 orbiter remained successful - it continues to orbit the Moon and send back valuable scientific data.

A is incorrect: Chandrayaan-2 studied the Moon, not Venus.

C is incorrect: It did not carry astronauts.

D is incorrect: It was a lunar mission, not India's first Mars mission (that was Mangalyaan).

💡 The Chandrayaan-2 orbiter carries a high-resolution camera and instruments still mapping the lunar surface.
Q.No 35 Science & TechnologyHindi
भारत सरकार द्वारा 2025 में शुरू की गई कृत्रिम बुद्धिमत्ता कार्य योजना (808 पा 6॥॥पुहा06€ 80000 98) का नाम कया है?
A
इंडिया-एआई मिशन
✓ Correct
B
डिजिटल एआई इंडिया प्लान
C
भारत एआई स्ट्रेटेजी
D
एआई इंडिया विज़न 2040

Solution

Correct answer: A. The IndiaAI Mission is the artificial-intelligence programme launched by the Government of India (approved 2024) to build AI compute, datasets and skilling capacity.

B is incorrect: 'Digital AI India Plan' is not the official name.

C is incorrect: 'Bharat AI Strategy' is not the launched mission's name.

D is incorrect: 'AI India Vision 2040' is not the official programme.

💡 The IndiaAI Mission aims to develop a sovereign AI ecosystem, including affordable computing power for startups and researchers.
Q.No 28 Science & TechnologyHindi
किसी विद्यार्थी को बारिश के ठीक बाद इंद्रधनुष दिखाई देता है। यदि वह इंद्रधनुष के निकट जाने की कोशिश करे, तो क्या होगा?
A
वह इंद्रधनुष तक पहुंच जाएगा
B
इंद्रधनुष अदृश्य हो जाएगा
C
इंद्रधनुष समान दूरी पर दिखाई देगा
✓ Correct
D
इंद्रधनुष रंग बदलेगा

Solution

Correct answer: C. A rainbow is an optical effect formed at a fixed angle relative to the observer; as the student moves towards it, it appears to stay at the same distance and can never be reached.

A is incorrect: One cannot actually reach a rainbow.

B is incorrect: The rainbow does not vanish simply because one moves towards it.

D is incorrect: The colours do not change as one approaches.

💡 A rainbow forms because raindrops refract, internally reflect and disperse sunlight at about a 42-degree angle.
Q.No 30 Science & TechnologyHindi
पृथ्वी द्वारा सूर्य के प्रकाश को चंद्रमा तक पहुंचने से रोकने के परिणामस्वरूप कौन-सी खगोलीय घटना होती है?
A
सूर्य ग्रहण
B
चंद्र ग्रहण
✓ Correct
C
उल्का वृष्टि
D
उत्तर ध्रुवीय ज्योति

Solution

Correct answer: B. A lunar eclipse occurs when the Earth comes between the Sun and the Moon, blocking sunlight from reaching the Moon and casting Earth's shadow on it.

A is incorrect: A solar eclipse occurs when the Moon blocks sunlight from reaching the Earth.

C is incorrect: A meteor shower is caused by debris burning in the atmosphere, unrelated to shadows.

D is incorrect: The aurora (northern lights) is caused by solar particles in the atmosphere.

💡 A lunar eclipse can occur only on a full-moon night when Sun, Earth and Moon are aligned.
Q.No 33 Science & TechnologyHindi
हृदू पेशियों को के रूप में वर्गीकृत किया जाता है।
A
ऐच्छिक पेशियों
B
अनैच्छिक पेशियों
✓ Correct
C
रेखित कंकाल पेशियों
D
चिकनी पेशियों

Solution

Correct answer: B. Cardiac (heart) muscles are classified as involuntary muscles because they contract automatically without conscious control.

A is incorrect: They are not under voluntary control.

C is incorrect: Skeletal muscles are voluntary and striated; cardiac muscle, though striated, is involuntary.

D is incorrect: Smooth muscle is involuntary too, but heart muscle is specifically cardiac muscle.

💡 Cardiac muscle is unique: it is striated like skeletal muscle yet involuntary like smooth muscle, and never tires.
Q.No 34 Science & TechnologyHindi
कौन-सी ग्रंथि, वृद्धि हार्मोन स्रावित करती है?
A
थाइरॉयड ग्रंथि
B
अधिवृक्क ग्रंथि
C
पीयूष ग्रंथि
✓ Correct
D
अग्न्याशय

Solution

Correct answer: C. The pituitary gland secretes growth hormone (somatotropin), which controls body growth and development.

A is incorrect: The thyroid gland secretes thyroxine, which regulates metabolism.

B is incorrect: The adrenal gland secretes adrenaline and cortisol.

D is incorrect: The pancreas secretes insulin and digestive enzymes.

💡 The pituitary is called the 'master gland' because it controls several other endocrine glands.
Q.No 24 Science & TechnologyHindi
एक सामान्य प्रकार के बाइफोकल लेंस में होते हैं
A
दो अवतल लेंस
B
दो उत्तल लेंस
C
एक अवतल और एक उत्तल लेंस
✓ Correct
D
दो बेलनाकार लेंस

Solution

Correct answer: C. A common bifocal lens has two parts - one concave (for distant vision) and one convex (for near reading) - combined in a single lens.

A is incorrect: Two concave lenses would not correct near vision.

B is incorrect: Two convex lenses would not correct distant vision for such a defect.

D is incorrect: Two cylindrical lenses are used to correct astigmatism, not in standard bifocals.

💡 Bifocal lenses were famously invented by Benjamin Franklin.
Q.No 27 Science & TechnologyHindi
भूस्थिरीय कक्ष में एक उपग्रह, पृथ्वी की भूमध्य रेखा (६५७०६०) पर किसी बिंदु के ऊपर स्थिर रहता है। उपग्रह की अक्षीय त्रिज्या लगभग 42,200 किमी है। उपग्रह की कक्षीय अवधि घंटे में परिकलित कीजिए।
A
24 घंटे
✓ Correct
B
12 de
C
6 घंटे
D
48 घंटे

Solution

Correct answer: A. A geostationary satellite stays fixed above a point on the equator, which requires an orbital period equal to the Earth's rotation - about 24 hours.

B is incorrect: 12 hours corresponds to a lower (e.g. GPS-type) orbit.

C is incorrect: 6 hours is far too short for geostationary orbit.

D is incorrect: 48 hours is twice the Earth's rotation period.

💡 Geostationary satellites orbit at about 35,786 km above the equator and are widely used for communication and weather monitoring.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyEnglish
मनुष्यों में कुल 46 गुणसूत्र होते हैं। इनमें से कितने अलैंगिक गुणसूत्र (अलिंग गुणसूत्र) युग्म होते हैं?
A
20
B
22
✓ Correct
C
23
D
21

Solution

Correct answer: B. Humans have 46 chromosomes - 23 pairs - of which 22 pairs are autosomes (non-sex chromosomes) and one pair is the sex chromosomes.

A is incorrect: 20 is fewer than the number of autosome pairs.

C is incorrect: 23 is the total number of pairs, not the number of autosomal pairs.

D is incorrect: 21 is one short of the correct number.

💡 The 23rd pair (XX or XY) determines biological sex.
Q.No 22 Science & TechnologyHindi
मुक्त गिरावट के दौरान (वायु प्रतिरोध की उपेक्षा करते हुए) किसी वस्तु के जमीन से टकराने से ठीक पहले, उसकी स्थितिज और गतिज ऊर्जाएं किस प्रकार संबंधित होती हैं?
A
स्थितिज ऊर्जा अधिकतम है, गतिज ऊर्जा न्यूनतम है
B
स्थितिज ऊर्जा न्यूनतम, गतिज ऊर्जा अधिकतम
✓ Correct
C
गतिज और स्थितिज ऊर्जा दोनों शून्य हैं
D
परिवर्तन के कारण कुछ ऊर्जा नष्ट हो जाती है

Solution

Correct answer: B. In free fall (ignoring air resistance), just before hitting the ground the object's potential energy is at a minimum and its kinetic energy is at a maximum, as PE converts into KE.

A is incorrect: PE is minimum (not maximum) at the lowest point.

C is incorrect: Both energies are not zero; KE is maximum.

D is incorrect: In ideal free fall total mechanical energy is conserved, so no energy is lost.

💡 This illustrates conservation of mechanical energy: PE + KE remains constant throughout the fall.
Q.No 25 Science & TechnologyHindi
यदि उपग्रह की कक्षीय अवधि 90 मिनट है, तो उसे कक्षा में बनाए रखने वाला प्रमुख बल क्या होगा?
A
पृथ्वी का गुरुत्वाकर्षण बल
✓ Correct
B
उपग्रह का अपकेंद्री बल
C
पृथ्वी से चुंबकीय बल
D
विद्युत चुंबकीय बल

Solution

Correct answer: A. The main force that keeps a satellite in orbit (period 90 minutes) is the Earth's gravitational force, which provides the necessary centripetal force.

B is incorrect: The 'centrifugal force' is a pseudo-force in the rotating frame, not the real force holding the satellite.

C is incorrect: Earth's magnetic force is far too weak to hold a satellite in orbit.

D is incorrect: Electromagnetic force is not what keeps a satellite orbiting.

💡 In orbit, gravity continuously pulls the satellite towards Earth, bending its path into a closed orbit.
Q.No 26 Science & TechnologyEnglish⚠ low OCR confidence
यदि चंद्रमा को पृथ्वी की परिक्रमा करने में 27 दिन लगते हैं, तो वह अपनी कक्षा में प्रतिदिन कितने डिग्री घूमता है?
A
13.3 degrees
✓ Correct
B
27 degrees
C
360 degrees
D
7 degrees

Solution

Correct answer: A. If the Moon completes its orbit (360 degrees) in about 27 days, it moves roughly 360/27 = 13.3 degrees per day.

B is incorrect: 27 degrees is the number of days, not the daily angular motion.

C is incorrect: 360 degrees is the full orbit, covered over the whole 27 days.

D is incorrect: 7 degrees per day would imply an orbit much slower than 27 days.

💡 This daily shift is why the Moon rises roughly 50 minutes later each day.
Q.No 27 Science & TechnologyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
किस भारतीय उपग्रह ने हुदहुद और फैलिन जैसे चक्रवातों की निगरानी में मदद की?
A
INSAT-3D
✓ Correct
B
Megha-Tropiques-1
C
GSAT-7
D
RISAT-2

Solution

Correct answer: A. The INSAT-3D meteorological satellite helped monitor cyclones such as Hudhud and Phailin, providing weather imaging and early warnings.

B is incorrect: Megha-Tropiques studies the tropical water cycle but is not the cyclone-tracking satellite referenced.

C is incorrect: GSAT-7 is a naval communication satellite.

D is incorrect: RISAT-2 is a radar imaging satellite used mainly for surveillance.

💡 INSAT-3D carries an imager and a sounder used by the India Meteorological Department for forecasting.
Q.No 25 Science & TechnologyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
पृथ्वी और चंद्रमा के बीच औसत दूरी 384,000 किमी है। चंद्रमा का व्यास लगभग 3,474 किमी है। पृथ्वी से देखे गए चंद्रमा के कोणीय व्यास का परिकलन (रेडियन में) कीजिए।
A
0.009 रेडियन
✓ Correct
B
0.008 रेडियन
C
0.010 ¢fsaq
D
0.007 रेडियन

Solution

Correct answer: A. Angular diameter (in radians) = actual diameter / distance = 3,474 / 384,000 = about 0.009 radian.

B is incorrect: 0.008 radian is slightly too small for this calculation.

C is incorrect: 0.010 radian is a little too large.

D is incorrect: 0.007 radian is too small.

💡 This tiny angular size (about half a degree) is why the Moon and Sun appear almost the same size in our sky.
Q.No 26 Science & TechnologyHindi
300 किमी की ऊंचाई पर, किसी उपग्रह की कक्षीय चाल 7.8 किमी/सेकंड है। यदि उपग्रह की ऊंचाई में 200 किमी की वृद्धि होती है, तो उसकी कक्षीय अवधि पर क्या प्रभाव पड़ेगा?
A
इसमें वृद्धि होगी
✓ Correct
B
इसमें कमी होगी
C
कोई परिवर्तन नहीं होगा
D
यादृच्छिक रूप से बदलता रहेगा

Solution

Correct answer: A. If a satellite's altitude increases, its orbital radius grows, so its orbital period increases (by Kepler's third law, a larger orbit takes longer).

B is incorrect: The period increases, not decreases, with greater altitude.

C is incorrect: There is a definite change; it does not stay the same.

D is incorrect: The change is systematic (an increase), not random.

💡 Higher orbits are slower: a satellite farther from Earth travels at a lower speed and takes longer per revolution.
Q.No 27 Science & TechnologyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
65।/ ।त ॥॥ (/५3) की ८0 तक 4000 0 पेलोड क्षमता है। यदि यह 3600 ४0 का उपग्रह लॉन्च करता है, और दक्षता 90% है, तो वास्तविक ऊर्जा आदर्श ऊर्जा की तुलना में कितनी उपयोग की गई है ?
A
1.1 times
✓ Correct
B
1.2 times
C
1.0 times
D
0.9 times

Solution

Correct answer: A. With 90% efficiency, the actual energy used is the ideal energy divided by 0.9, i.e. about 1.1 times the ideal energy (more input is needed to account for losses).

B is incorrect: 1.2 times would correspond to about 83% efficiency.

C is incorrect: 1.0 times would mean 100% efficiency (no losses).

D is incorrect: 0.9 times would wrongly imply less energy than ideal is used.

💡 Efficiency = useful output / total input, so lower efficiency means more input energy is required.
Q.No 29 Science & TechnologyEnglish⚠ low OCR confidence
कौन-सा इसरो उपग्रह भारतीय नौसेना की संचार संबंधी आवश्यकताओं का समर्थन करता है?
A
GSAT-12
B
GSAT-7
✓ Correct
C
GSAT-29
D
Cartosat-2

Solution

Correct answer: B. GSAT-7 (Rukmini) is the ISRO satellite that supports the Indian Navy's communication needs, providing secure connectivity over the maritime region.

A is incorrect: GSAT-12 is a general communication satellite, not the naval one.

C is incorrect: GSAT-29 is a high-throughput communication satellite for remote areas.

D is incorrect: Cartosat-2 is an Earth-imaging satellite, not a communication satellite.

💡 GSAT-7A, a related satellite, similarly supports the Indian Air Force.
Q.No 32 Science & TechnologyHindi
नाइट्रोजन वायुमंडल में अक्रिय क्यों है?
A
क्योंकि यह पराबैंगनी किरणों को अवशोषित कर लेती है।
B
क्योंकि यह आसानी से अभिक्रिया नहीं करती।
✓ Correct
C
क्योंकि यह दुर्लभ है।
D
क्योंकि यह ओज़ोन का निर्माण करती है।

Solution

Correct answer: B. Nitrogen is largely inert in the atmosphere because its molecules (N2) have a very strong triple bond, so it does not react easily under normal conditions.

A is incorrect: Nitrogen does not chiefly absorb ultraviolet rays; that is ozone.

C is incorrect: Nitrogen is abundant (about 78% of air), not rare.

D is incorrect: Nitrogen does not form ozone.

💡 The strong N=N triple bond must be broken (e.g. by lightning or special bacteria) for nitrogen to combine with other elements.
Q.No 40 Science & TechnologyHindi
धारा प्रवाहित करने वाला एक आयताकार लूप किसी चुंबकीय क्षेत्र में स्वतंत्र रूप से लटका हुआ है। यह स्वाभाविक रूप से किस स्थिति में पहुँचेगा?
A
क्षेत्र रेखाओं के लंबवत
B
यादृच्छिक अभिविन्यास
C
क्षेत्र रेखाओं के समानांतर
✓ Correct
D
कोई निश्चित स्थिति नहीं है

Solution

Correct answer: C. A freely suspended current-carrying rectangular loop in a magnetic field rotates until its plane lies along (parallel to) the field lines - i.e. its magnetic moment aligns with the field - which is its stable equilibrium.

A is incorrect: With its plane perpendicular to the field lines the loop experiences maximum torque, so it does not stay there.

B is incorrect: It settles into a definite orientation, not a random one.

D is incorrect: There is a definite stable position, contrary to this option.

💡 This aligning torque on a current loop is the basic principle behind moving-coil galvanometers and electric motors.
Q.No 24 Science & TechnologyEnglish
What is the frequency range of sound that can be heard by the human ear?
A
2 Hz to 200 Hz
B
20 Hz to 20,000 Hz
✓ Correct
C
200 Hz to 2000 Hz
D
2000 Hz to 20,000 Hz

Solution

Correct answer: B. The human ear can hear sound in the frequency range of about 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz (20 kHz).

A is incorrect: 2-200 Hz is far below the full audible range.

C is incorrect: 200-2000 Hz is only a part of the audible range.

D is incorrect: 2000-20,000 Hz covers only the higher part of the range.

💡 Sound below 20 Hz is called infrasound and above 20 kHz is ultrasound.
Q.No 30 Science & TechnologyEnglish⚠ low OCR confidence
Which element is required by the thyroid gland to produce thyroxine?
A
Calcium
B
iodine
✓ Correct
C
Sodium
D
Potassium

Solution

Correct answer: B. The thyroid gland requires iodine to produce the hormone thyroxine (T4).

A is incorrect: Calcium is needed for bones and is regulated by other hormones.

C is incorrect: Sodium is an electrolyte, not the element in thyroxine.

D is incorrect: Potassium is an electrolyte, not used to make thyroxine.

💡 Iodine deficiency causes goitre; iodised salt is used to prevent it.
Q.No 25 Science & TechnologyHindi
यदि चंद्रमा, पृथ्वी की सतह पर 0.5" का कोण अंतरित करता है, तो इसके व्यास का परिकलन कीजिए, दिया गया है कि पृथ्वी से इसकी औसत दूरी 3.8:«0” मीटर है।
A
3500 किमी
B
3475 किमी
✓ Correct
C
3000 किमी
D
4000 किमी

Solution

Correct answer: B. Using diameter = angular size (in radians) x distance: 0.5 degrees is about 0.0087 rad; 0.0087 x 3.8x10^8 m gives roughly 3,475 km, the Moon's diameter.

A is incorrect: 3500 km is close but the precise value is about 3,475 km.

C is incorrect: 3000 km is too small.

D is incorrect: 4000 km is too large.

💡 The Moon's actual diameter is about 3,474 km, roughly a quarter of Earth's.
Q.No 26 Science & TechnologyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
aleux Yet (Kuiper Belt) |
A
नेपच्यून से परे बर्फीले पिंडों से भरा एक क्षेत्र है
✓ Correct
B
मंगल और बृहस्पति के बीच क्षुद्रग्रह घेरा है
C
धूमकेतु की पूंछ है
D
सूर्य के चारों ओर का वह क्षेत्र है जो सौर पवन उत्पन्न करता है

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Kuiper Belt is a region of icy bodies beyond the orbit of Neptune, including dwarf planets like Pluto.

B is incorrect: The asteroid belt (rocky bodies) lies between Mars and Jupiter.

C is incorrect: It is a region of objects, not a comet's tail.

D is incorrect: It is not the source region of the solar wind (that is the Sun).

💡 Many short-period comets originate from the Kuiper Belt.
Q.No 27 Science & TechnologyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
किस भारतीय मिशन ने भारत को अपने पहले प्रयास में ही मंगल की कक्षा में सफलतापूर्वक पहुंचने वाला पहला देश बना दिया?
A
PSLV-1
B
Mangalyaan (MOM)
✓ Correct
C
Chandrayaan-2
D
Aditya-L1

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan) made India the first country to reach Mars orbit on its very first attempt (2014).

A is incorrect: The PSLV is a launch vehicle, not the Mars mission itself.

C is incorrect: Chandrayaan-2 was a lunar mission.

D is incorrect: Aditya-L1 is India's solar mission.

💡 Mangalyaan was also one of the most cost-effective interplanetary missions ever flown.
Q.No 30 Science & TechnologyHindi
वायुमंडल में ऑक्सीजन की पूर्ति मुख्यतः द्वारा होती है।
A
प्रकाश संश्लेषण
✓ Correct
B
दहन
C
औद्योगिक गतिविधियाँ
D
श्ुसन

Solution

Correct answer: A. Oxygen in the atmosphere is replenished mainly through photosynthesis by plants, algae and cyanobacteria.

B is incorrect: Combustion consumes oxygen rather than producing it.

C is incorrect: Industrial activities consume oxygen and emit pollutants.

D is incorrect: Respiration also consumes oxygen and releases carbon dioxide.

💡 Photosynthesis uses carbon dioxide and water with sunlight to produce glucose and release oxygen.
Q.No 35 Science & TechnologyHindi
एक आवेशित कण एकसमान चुंबकीय क्षेत्र में क्षेत्र के लंबवत वेग से प्रवेश करता है। इसकी गति की प्रकृति क्या है?
A
सीधी रेखा
B
वृत्ताकार पथ
✓ Correct
C
कुंडलित पथ
D
यादृच्छिक गति

Solution

Correct answer: B. A charged particle entering a uniform magnetic field perpendicular to it moves in a circular path, because the magnetic force always acts perpendicular to its velocity.

A is incorrect: It does not move in a straight line; a force acts on it.

C is incorrect: A helical (spiral) path occurs when the velocity has a component along the field, not perpendicular.

D is incorrect: The motion is well-defined (circular), not random.

💡 This circular motion is the basis of devices like the cyclotron.
Q.No 28 Science & TechnologyHindi
अस्थि की कठोरता मुख्यतः: किसकी उपस्थिति के कारण होती है?
A
आयरन और मैग्नीशियम यौगिक
B
कैल्शियम और फास्फोरस यौगिक
✓ Correct
C
सोडियम और पोटेशियम यौगिक
D
सल्फर और क्लोराइड यौगिक

Solution

Correct answer: B. The hardness of bone is mainly due to the presence of calcium and phosphorus compounds (calcium phosphate) in its matrix.

A is incorrect: Iron and magnesium compounds are not the main hardening minerals of bone.

C is incorrect: Sodium and potassium are electrolytes, not bone-hardening minerals.

D is incorrect: Sulphur and chloride compounds do not give bone its hardness.

💡 Bone combines a hard mineral (hydroxyapatite) with flexible collagen fibres.
Q.No 36 Science & TechnologyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
निम्नलिखित में से कौन दहन का एक प्रकार है? [. तीव्र दहन [. स्वतः ज्वलन
A
Only I
B
Only II
C
Both I and II
✓ Correct
D
Neither I nor II

Solution

Correct answer: C. Both rapid combustion (I) and spontaneous combustion (II) are recognised types of combustion, so 'Both I and II' is correct.

A is incorrect: 'Only I' leaves out spontaneous combustion.

B is incorrect: 'Only II' leaves out rapid combustion.

D is incorrect: 'Neither' is wrong since both are types of combustion.

💡 Other types include slow combustion and explosive combustion.
Q.No 21 Science & TechnologyHindi
जीव-रूपों में कार्बन का समावेश के माध्यम से होता है।
A
श्ुसन
B
प्रकाश संश्लेषण
✓ Correct
C
किण्वन
D
अपघटन

Solution

Correct answer: B. Carbon is incorporated into living organisms through photosynthesis, in which plants fix atmospheric carbon dioxide into organic compounds (glucose).

A is incorrect: Respiration releases carbon dioxide rather than fixing it into organisms.

C is incorrect: Fermentation is a form of respiration that releases carbon dioxide.

D is incorrect: Decomposition returns carbon to the environment, it does not incorporate it into living bodies.

💡 Photosynthesis is the entry point of carbon into the food chain.
Q.No 25 Science & TechnologyHindi
"प्रकाश वर्ष" शब्द को सर्वोत्तम ढंग से किस प्रकार परिभाषित किया जा सकता है?
A
प्रकाश द्वारा एक घंटे में दूरी तय करने में लिया गया समय
B
प्रकाश द्वारा एक वर्ष में तय की गई दूरी
✓ Correct
C
प्रकाश की चाल ५ समय
D
एक परिक्रमा पूर्ण करने में पृथ्वी द्वारा लिया गया समय

Solution

Correct answer: B. A 'light year' is defined as the distance that light travels in one year - it is a unit of distance, not time.

A is incorrect: It is not the distance covered in one hour.

C is incorrect: 'Speed x time' is a general formula, not the precise definition of a light year.

D is incorrect: The time for one revolution of the Earth is a year, not a light year.

💡 One light year is about 9.46 trillion kilometres.
Q.No 26 Science & TechnologyHindi
अंतरिक्ष से आने वाली रेडियो तरंगों को देखने के लिए किस प्रकार के दूरदर्शक का उपयोग किया जाता है?
A
प्रकाशीय दूरदर्शक
B
परावर्ती टूरदर्शक
C
रेडियो दूरदर्शक
✓ Correct
D
अपयवर्ती दूरदर्शक

Solution

Correct answer: C. A radio telescope is used to observe radio waves coming from space, allowing study of objects invisible to optical telescopes.

A is incorrect: An optical telescope collects visible light.

B is incorrect: A reflecting telescope is a type of optical telescope.

D is incorrect: A refracting telescope is also an optical telescope using lenses.

💡 India's Giant Metrewave Radio Telescope (GMRT) near Pune is one of the world's largest radio telescope arrays.
Q.No 34 Science & TechnologyHindi
दो लंबे समानांतर तार एक ही दिशा में धारा प्रवाहित करते हैं। उनके बीच क्या होता है?
A
वे एक दूसरे को प्रतिकर्षित करते हैं
B
वे एक दूसरे को आकर्षित करते हैं
✓ Correct
C
वे अप्रभावित रहते हैं
D
वे दोलन करते हैं

Solution

Correct answer: B. Two long parallel wires carrying current in the same direction attract each other, due to the interaction of their magnetic fields.

A is incorrect: They attract, not repel, when currents flow in the same direction.

C is incorrect: They are not unaffected; there is a definite force.

D is incorrect: They do not oscillate under this steady force.

💡 Currents in opposite directions repel each other; this effect is used to define the ampere.
Q.No 38 Science & TechnologyHindi
प्रोजेक्ट आकाशतीर क्या है?
A
ड्रोन हमलावर
B
स्वचालित वायु रक्षा नियंत्रण
✓ Correct
C
लेजर शील्ड प्रणाली
D
ए.आई. आधारित रॉकेट लांचर

Solution

Correct answer: B. Project Akashteer is an automated air-defence control and reporting system for the Indian Army, integrating radars and sensors to manage the airspace.

A is incorrect: It is not a drone attacker.

C is incorrect: It is not a laser shield system.

D is incorrect: It is not an AI-based rocket launcher.

💡 Akashteer enables coordinated, automated control of low-level airspace and air-defence weapons.
Q.No 39 Science & TechnologyHindi
अप्रैल 2025 में, सिकल सेल रोग (एस.सी.डी) की प्रारंभिक जांच के लिए एक किफायती इलेक्ट्रो-फुइडडिक उपकरण किस भारतीय अनुसंधान संस्थान द्वारा विकसित किया गया था?
A
टाटा मेमोरियल सेंटर
B
रमन अनुसंधान संस्थान
✓ Correct
C
वैज्ञानिक और औद्योगिक अनुसंधान परिषद
D
अखिल भारतीय आयुर्विज्ञान संस्थान

Solution

Correct answer: B. In April 2025, the Raman Research Institute developed an affordable electro-fluidic device for the early screening of Sickle Cell Disease (SCD).

A is incorrect: Tata Memorial Centre focuses on cancer care, not this device.

C is incorrect: CSIR is a broad research body, but the device was credited to the Raman Research Institute.

D is incorrect: AIIMS is a medical institute but not the developer cited here.

💡 India runs a National Sickle Cell Anaemia Elimination Mission targeting tribal and vulnerable populations.
Q.No 28 Science & TechnologyHindi
मनुष्यों में किस प्रकार के ग्राही, गंध का पता लगाने के लिए उत्तरदायी होते हैं?
A
घ्राण ग्राही
✓ Correct
B
रससंवेदी ग्राही
C
श्रवण ग्राही
D
दृश्य ग्राही

Solution

Correct answer: A. Olfactory receptors are responsible for detecting smell in humans, located in the lining of the nasal cavity.

B is incorrect: Gustatory receptors detect taste.

C is incorrect: Auditory receptors detect sound.

D is incorrect: Visual receptors (in the retina) detect light.

💡 Olfactory (smell) and gustatory (taste) senses work together to give us flavour.
Q.No 36 Science & TechnologyHindi
जब पराध्वानी तरंगों को सफाई के विलयन में भेजा जाता है तो धूल, चिकनाई तथा गंदगी के कणों का क्या होता हैं?
A
वे वाष्पित हो जाते हैं
B
वे घुल जाते हैं
C
वे अलग होकर निचे गिर जाते हैं
✓ Correct
D
वे जल जाते हैं

Solution

Correct answer: C. When ultrasonic (ultrasound) waves are passed through a cleaning solution, the vibration shakes loose dust, grease and dirt particles, which separate and fall away from the object.

A is incorrect: They do not evaporate.

B is incorrect: They do not dissolve.

D is incorrect: They are not burnt off.

💡 Ultrasonic cleaning works through 'cavitation' - tiny bubbles that scrub surfaces clean.
Q.No 23 Science & TechnologyEnglish⚠ low OCR confidence
यदि किसी ग्रह को सूर्य की परिक्रमा करने में 88 पृथ्वी दिन लगते हैं, तो सेकंड में उसकी कक्षीय अवधि क्या होगी?
A
7.6 x 10^6 s
✓ Correct
B
6.8 x 10^6 s
C
7.1 x 10^6 s
D
7.9 x 10^6 s

Solution

Correct answer: A. 88 Earth days x 86,400 seconds/day = about 7.6 x 10^6 seconds, so the orbital period is roughly 7.6 x 10^6 s.

B is incorrect: 6.8 x 10^6 s is too small for 88 days.

C is incorrect: 7.1 x 10^6 s does not match 88 days.

D is incorrect: 7.9 x 10^6 s is slightly too large.

💡 A planet with an 88-day orbit is Mercury, the closest planet to the Sun.
Q.No 24 Science & TechnologyHindi
किस ग्रह को तूफानी ग्रह (5(00४ 0।806!) कहा जाता है?
A
बुध
B
बृहस्पति
✓ Correct
C
मंगल
D
शुक्र

Solution

Correct answer: B. Jupiter is called the 'storm planet' because of its enormous, long-lived storms, most famously the Great Red Spot.

A is incorrect: Mercury has no thick atmosphere to sustain storms.

C is incorrect: Mars has dust storms but is not the 'storm planet'.

D is incorrect: Venus has thick clouds but is known as the hottest planet, not the storm planet.

💡 Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a storm larger than the Earth that has raged for centuries.
Q.No 28 Science & TechnologyHindi
किस दूरदर्शी को 'फाइबर ऑप्टिक्स का जनक' कहा जाता है?
A
कुणाल कपूर
B
विवेक सहगल
C
नरिंदर सिंह कपानी
✓ Correct
D
जय चौधरी

Solution

Correct answer: C. Narinder Singh Kapany, an Indian-American physicist, is called the 'Father of Fibre Optics' for his pioneering work on transmitting light through glass fibres.

A is incorrect: Kunal Kapoor is not associated with fibre optics.

B is incorrect: Vivek Sahgal is not the fibre-optics pioneer.

D is incorrect: Jay Chaudhry is a cybersecurity entrepreneur, not the fibre-optics pioneer.

💡 Kapany coined the term 'fibre optics' in the 1950s.
Q.No 34 Science & TechnologyHindi
नवंबर 2024 में भारतीय सेना द्वारा अधिग्रहित इलेक्ट्रिक मानवरहित हेलीकॉप्टर सबल 20 का विकास किस संस्थान ने किया था?
A
आई.आई.टी. बॉम्बे
B
आई.आई.टी. दिल्ली
C
आई.आई.टी. कानपुर
✓ Correct
D
आई.आई.टी. मद्रास

Solution

Correct answer: C. The electric unmanned helicopter 'Sabal 20', inducted by the Indian Army in November 2024, was developed by IIT Kanpur.

A is incorrect: IIT Bombay did not develop Sabal 20.

B is incorrect: IIT Delhi was not the developer.

D is incorrect: IIT Madras was not the developer.

💡 Such unmanned helicopters can carry supplies to forward or high-altitude posts.
Q.No 35 Science & TechnologyHindi
जनवरी 2025 में हुए घोषणा के अनुसार, किस संगठन ने स्वदेशी रूप से उत्पादित 'पैरासिटामोल' विकसित किया है?
A
वैज्ञानिक तथा औद्योगिक अनुसंधान परिषद
✓ Correct
B
अखिल भारतीय आयुर्विज्ञान संस्थान
C
इंदिरा गांधी मेडिकल कॉलेज एवं अस्पताल
D
जुबली मिशन मेडिकल कॉलेज और अनुसंधान संस्थान

Solution

Correct answer: A. As announced in January 2025, the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) developed an indigenously produced 'paracetamol' (with a domestic route for its key raw material).

B is incorrect: AIIMS is a medical institute, not the developer.

C is incorrect: Indira Gandhi Medical College is not credited with this.

D is incorrect: Jubilee Mission Medical College is not the developer.

💡 Reducing import dependence for drug raw materials (APIs) is a key goal of India's bulk-drug policy.
Q.No 38 Science & TechnologyHindi
फ़ेमिंग के वामहस्त नियम के अनुसार, निम्नलिखित में से किसे बाएं हाथ की दूसरी उंगली का उपयोग करके दर्शाया जाता है?
A
विद्युत धारा की दिशा
✓ Correct
B
चुंबकीय क्षेत्र की दिशा
C
चालक पर कार्य करने वाला बल
D
प्रतिरोध का माप

Solution

Correct answer: A. By Fleming's left-hand rule, the second finger (the forefinger is the field) - i.e. the middle/second finger - represents the direction of the electric current.

B is incorrect: The forefinger (first finger) represents the magnetic field.

C is incorrect: The thumb represents the force/motion on the conductor.

D is incorrect: Fleming's rule does not represent resistance.

💡 Fleming's left-hand rule is used for motors; the right-hand rule is used for generators.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyEnglish
Which of the following is an example of voluntary action seen in humans?
A
Vomiting
B
Salivation
C
Writing
✓ Correct
D
Breathing

Solution

Correct answer: C. Writing is a voluntary action - it is performed consciously under the control of the will.

A is incorrect: Vomiting is a reflex/involuntary action.

B is incorrect: Salivation is an involuntary (reflex) response.

D is incorrect: Breathing is largely involuntary (though it can be briefly controlled).

💡 Voluntary actions are controlled by the cerebrum (the conscious part of the brain).
Q.No 36 Science & TechnologyEnglish
What is the primary function of a fire extinguisher?
A
To add more fuel to the fire
B
To increase the temperature of the fuel
C
To increase the supply of air
D
To cut off the supply of air, reduce the temperature of the fuel, or both
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. A fire extinguisher works by cutting off the air (oxygen) supply, reducing the temperature of the fuel, or both - removing the conditions needed for combustion.

A is incorrect: It does not add fuel to the fire.

B is incorrect: It does not raise the fuel's temperature.

C is incorrect: It does not increase the air supply.

💡 The 'fire triangle' needs fuel, heat and oxygen; removing any one puts out the fire.
Q.No 25 Science & TechnologyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
वायु में जल को के रूप में मापा जाता है।
A
आर्द्रता
✓ Correct
B
घनत्व
C
ताप
D
दाब

Solution

Correct answer: A. The amount of water (vapour) present in air is measured as humidity.

B is incorrect: Density measures mass per unit volume, not water content of air.

C is incorrect: Temperature measures heat, not water content.

D is incorrect: Pressure measures force per unit area, not humidity.

💡 Relative humidity is the ratio of actual water vapour to the maximum the air can hold at that temperature.
Q.No 32 Science & TechnologyHindi
कक्षा पर वह बिंदु है जहां पृथ्वी की सतह से उपग्रह की ऊर्ध्वधिर दूरी लघुतम होती है।
A
अपभू
B
उपभू
✓ Correct
C
आनति
D
लैग्रेंज बिंदु

Solution

Correct answer: B. The 'perigee' is the point on a satellite's orbit where its vertical distance from the Earth's surface is the least (closest approach).

A is incorrect: The apogee is the farthest point from Earth.

C is incorrect: 'Inclination' is the tilt of the orbit, not a distance point.

D is incorrect: A Lagrange point is an equilibrium point in a two-body system, not the closest orbital point.

💡 For objects orbiting the Sun, the corresponding closest point is the 'perihelion'.
Q.No 33 Science & TechnologyEnglish
भारतीय अंतरिक्ष स्टेशन में कितने मॉड्यूल होंगे?
A
3
B
4
C
5
✓ Correct
D
6

Solution

Correct answer: C. The planned Indian Space Station (Bharatiya Antariksha Station) is expected to have 5 modules.

A is incorrect: 3 is fewer than planned.

B is incorrect: 4 is not the planned number.

D is incorrect: 6 is more than planned.

💡 ISRO aims to build the station in stages, with the first module targeted later this decade.
Q.No 38 Science & TechnologyHindi
नाइट्रोजन और आर्गन जैसी रासायनिक रूप से निष्क्रिय गैसों को विद्युत बल्ब में भरने का प्राथमिक कारण क्या है?
A
बल्ब को भारी बनाने के लिए
B
प्रकाश की द्युति बढ़ाने के लिए
C
तंतु की आयु में वृद्धि करने के लिए
✓ Correct
D
उत्पादन लागत कम करने के लिए

Solution

Correct answer: C. Chemically inert gases like nitrogen and argon are filled in electric bulbs primarily to increase the life of the filament by preventing it from oxidising/evaporating quickly.

A is incorrect: They are not added to make the bulb heavier.

B is incorrect: They do not increase the brightness of the light.

D is incorrect: They do not reduce production cost.

💡 Without an inert gas, the hot tungsten filament would burn out rapidly in oxygen.
Q.No 40 Science & TechnologyHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा, कोक का उपयोग है?
A
आवासीय शीतलन के लिए ईधन के रूप में
B
खाद्य परिरक्षक के रूप में
C
फामस्यूटिकल्स के उत्पादन में
D
इस्पात के निर्माण में
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Coke (a carbon-rich fuel from coal) is used in the manufacture of steel, where it acts as a fuel and a reducing agent in the blast furnace.

A is incorrect: It is not used as a fuel for residential cooling.

B is incorrect: It is not a food preservative.

C is incorrect: It is not used to produce pharmaceuticals.

💡 In a blast furnace, coke reduces iron oxide ore to molten iron.
Q.No 28 Science & TechnologyHindi
पायरूवेट (माइटोकॉन्ड्रिया में) के वायवीय विखंडन में, निम्नलिखित में से क्या बनता है?
A
लैक्टिक अम्ल + ऊर्जा
B
कार्बन डाइऑक्साइड + जल + ऊर्जा
✓ Correct
C
एथेनॉल + कार्बन डाइऑक्साइड + ऊर्जा
D
लैक्टिक अम्ल + ऊर्जा + कार्बन डाइऑक्साइड

Solution

Correct answer: B. In the aerobic breakdown of pyruvate inside the mitochondria, the products are carbon dioxide, water and energy (ATP).

A is incorrect: Lactic acid forms in anaerobic respiration in muscles, not aerobic breakdown.

C is incorrect: Ethanol and CO2 form in anaerobic respiration in yeast (fermentation).

D is incorrect: Lactic acid with CO2 is not the aerobic product.

💡 Aerobic respiration yields far more ATP per glucose molecule than anaerobic respiration.
Q.No 34 Science & TechnologyHindi
श्रवण सहायक युक्ति का प्राथमिक कार्य क्या है?
A
शोर कम करना
B
ध्वनि तरंगों को प्रकाश संकेतों में परिवर्तित करना
C
स्पष्ट सुनने के लिए ध्वनि को बढ़ाना
✓ Correct
D
ध्वनि की आवृत्ति बदलना

Solution

Correct answer: C. The primary function of a hearing aid is to amplify sound so that it can be heard more clearly by a person with hearing loss.

A is incorrect: It does not merely reduce noise.

B is incorrect: It does not convert sound into light signals.

D is incorrect: It does not change the frequency of sound.

💡 A hearing aid has a microphone, an amplifier and a speaker.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyEnglish
ऑगस्ट 2025 पर्यतच्या माहितीनुसार, नासाने जवजवठत् परग्रहांची पुष्टी केली.
A
6000
✓ Correct
B
10000
C
12000
D
9800

Solution

Correct answer: A. As of around August 2025, NASA had confirmed roughly 6,000 exoplanets (planets outside our solar system).

B is incorrect: 10,000 overstates the confirmed count.

C is incorrect: 12,000 is far higher than confirmed.

D is incorrect: 9,800 overstates the number.

💡 Most confirmed exoplanets were discovered by the Kepler and TESS space telescopes.
Q.No 32 Science & TechnologyEnglish
मध्ये अंतराठ्यानाशी संपर्क तुटल्यानंतर चांद्रयान-। मिशन पूर्ण झाले.
A
2009
✓ Correct
B
2013
C
2017
D
2014

Solution

Correct answer: A. The Chandrayaan-1 mission ended in 2009 after contact with the spacecraft was lost.

B is incorrect: 2013 is the year Mangalyaan was launched, not when Chandrayaan-1 ended.

C is incorrect: 2017 is unrelated to Chandrayaan-1's end.

D is incorrect: 2014 is when Mangalyaan reached Mars.

💡 Despite ending early, Chandrayaan-1 confirmed the presence of water molecules on the Moon.
Q.No 33 Science & TechnologyHindi
फेब्रुवारी 2025 मध्ये, विद्यापीठात (0-00 ने विकसित केलेल्या 50 लेंबचे उद्घाटन करण्यात आले.
A
बेरहमपूर
B
गुवाहाटी
✓ Correct
C
मुंबई
D
जादवपूर

Solution

Correct answer: B. In February 2025, a 5G laboratory developed at a university was inaugurated at Guwahati (IIT Guwahati region).

A is incorrect: Berhampur was not the location.

C is incorrect: Mumbai was not the location cited.

D is incorrect: Jadavpur was not the location.

💡 5G test labs help researchers and startups develop and validate next-generation telecom applications.
Q.No 34 Science & TechnologyEnglish
5-400 क्षेपणास्त्र संरक्षण प्रणाली कोणत्या देशाने बनवली आहे?
A
रशिया (९७५५3)
✓ Correct
B
AREY (USA)
C
dq (China)
D
oat (Germany)

Solution

Correct answer: A. The S-400 missile-defence (air-defence) system is made by Russia.

B is incorrect: The USA makes systems like the Patriot/THAAD, not the S-400.

C is incorrect: China operates the S-400 (imported) but did not build it.

D is incorrect: Germany did not build the S-400.

💡 India signed a deal to buy S-400 systems from Russia for long-range air defence.
Q.No 38 Science & TechnologyEnglish
कं मीटर-बोर्डमध्ये, गीझर, एअर कूलर इत्यादी उच्च पॉवर रेटिंग असलेल्या उपकरणांसाठी खालीलपैकी कोणते धारा क्षमता निर्देशन (करंट रेटिंग) वापरले जाते?
A
4A
B
8A
C
11A
D
15A
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. High-power appliances such as geysers and air coolers use a 15 A current rating (and a corresponding socket), unlike low-power devices that use 5 A.

A is incorrect: 4 A is too low for high-power appliances.

B is incorrect: 8 A is not the standard high-power rating.

C is incorrect: 11 A is not the standard household high-power rating.

💡 Indian sockets commonly come in 5 A (light loads) and 15/16 A (heavy loads) ratings.
Q.No 40 Science & TechnologyHindi
खालीलपैकी कोणता दुर्मित्ठ मृदा मूलद्रव्य नाही? [. सेरियम '. युरोपियम
A
Only I
B
Only II
C
Both I and II
D
Neither I nor II
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Both Cerium (I) and Europium (II) ARE rare-earth elements, so neither of them is a 'non-rare-earth' element - hence 'Neither I nor II'.

A is incorrect: 'Only I' is wrong because Cerium is a rare-earth element.

B is incorrect: 'Only II' is wrong because Europium is a rare-earth element.

C is incorrect: 'Both I and II' would mean both are not rare-earth, which is incorrect.

💡 The rare-earth elements are the 15 lanthanides plus scandium and yttrium.
Q.No 30 Science & TechnologyHindi
मनुष्यों में वृद्धि हार्मोन-रिलीजिंग फैक्टर जारी करने के लिए कौन-सी मस्तिष्क संरचना उत्तरदायी होती है?
A
हाइपोथैंलेमस
✓ Correct
B
थायरॉयड ग्रंथि
C
पिट्यूटरी ग्रंथि
D
थाइमस ग्रंथि

Solution

Correct answer: A. The hypothalamus releases growth-hormone-releasing factor (hormone), which stimulates the pituitary to secrete growth hormone.

B is incorrect: The thyroid gland secretes thyroxine, not growth-hormone-releasing factor.

C is incorrect: The pituitary secretes growth hormone in response to the hypothalamus.

D is incorrect: The thymus is involved in immunity (T-cell maturation).

💡 The hypothalamus links the nervous and endocrine systems by controlling the pituitary gland.
Q.No 33 Science & TechnologyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
मानव शरीर के निम्नलिखित में से किस अंग का चित्रण पराध्वानी तरंगों का उपयोग करके नहीं किया जाता है? | सा [. पित्ताशय
A
Only I
B
Only II
C
Both I and II
D
Neither I nor II
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Both the heart (I) and the gallbladder (II) ARE routinely imaged using ultrasound (echocardiography and abdominal ultrasound), so neither is an organ that is not imaged - hence 'Neither I nor II'.

A is incorrect: 'Only I' is wrong because the heart is imaged by ultrasound.

B is incorrect: 'Only II' is wrong because the gallbladder is imaged by ultrasound.

C is incorrect: 'Both' would mean both are not imaged, which is incorrect.

💡 Ultrasound (sonography) uses high-frequency sound waves to image soft tissues and organs.
Q.No 22 Science & TechnologyEnglish
आर्गन का लगभग कितना प्रतिशत वायु में उपस्थित होता है?
A
0.03%
B
0.93%
✓ Correct
C
21%
D
78%

Solution

Correct answer: B. Argon makes up about 0.93% of the Earth's atmosphere, making it the third most abundant gas.

A is incorrect: 0.03% is roughly the share of carbon dioxide.

C is incorrect: 21% is the share of oxygen.

D is incorrect: 78% is the share of nitrogen.

💡 Argon is a noble (inert) gas used in light bulbs and welding.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyHindi
एक प्रकाश वर्ष का माप हैं।
A
समय
B
द्री
✓ Correct
C
चाल
D
द्रव्यमान

Solution

Correct answer: B. A light year is a measure of distance - the distance light travels in one year.

A is incorrect: It is not a measure of time.

C is incorrect: It is not a measure of speed.

D is incorrect: It is not a measure of mass.

💡 One light year is about 9.46 trillion kilometres.
Q.No 32 Science & TechnologyEnglish
निम्नलिखित में से किस वर्ष भारत ने अंतरिक्ष युग में प्रवेश किया, जब पहला भारतीय उपग्रह प्रश्नपित किया गया था?
A
1961
B
1971
C
1975
✓ Correct
D
1989

Solution

Correct answer: C. India entered the space age in 1975, when its first satellite, Aryabhata, was launched (with Soviet help).

A is incorrect: 1961 pre-dates India's first satellite.

B is incorrect: 1971 is incorrect.

D is incorrect: 1989 is much later.

💡 Aryabhata was named after the ancient Indian mathematician-astronomer.
Q.No 33 Science & TechnologyHindi
2024 में पूरी तरह से 30 प्रिंटेड इंजन वाला विश्व का पहला रॉकेट किसने लॉन्च किया था?
A
अग्रिकुल
✓ Correct
B
स्पेसएक्स
C
ब्लू ओरिजिन
D
स्काईरूट एयरोस्पेस

Solution

Correct answer: A. In 2024 Agnikul Cosmos launched the world's first rocket powered by a fully 3D-printed engine (the Agnibaan SOrTeD).

B is incorrect: SpaceX uses 3D-printed parts but did not achieve this first.

C is incorrect: Blue Origin did not launch this first.

D is incorrect: Skyroot Aerospace launched Vikram-S but not the 3D-printed-engine first.

💡 Agnikul is an Indian private space startup incubated at IIT Madras.
Q.No 38 Science & TechnologyHindi
विद्युत घंटी में सक्रिय होने के बाद विद्युत चुंबक क्या करता है?
A
लोहे की पट्टी को अपनी ओर खींचता है
✓ Correct
B
संपर्क पेंच खींचता हैं
C
घंटी बंद कर देता है
D
कुंडली पिघलाता है

Solution

Correct answer: A. In an electric bell, when activated the electromagnet pulls the iron strip (armature) towards it, which strikes the gong.

B is incorrect: It does not pull the contact screw towards it as the action that rings the bell.

C is incorrect: It does not switch the bell off (the make-and-break action keeps it ringing).

D is incorrect: It does not melt the coil.

💡 The repeated make-and-break of the circuit makes the hammer strike the gong continuously.
Q.No 40 Science & TechnologyHindi
निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा नाभिक सामान्यतः: नाभिकीय संलयन अभिक्रियाओं में उपयोग किया जाता हैं?
A
यूरेनियम और थोरियम
B
कार्बन और नाइट्रोजन
C
हाइड्रोजन और उसके समस्थानिक
✓ Correct
D
ऑक्सीजन और हीलियम

Solution

Correct answer: C. Nuclear fusion reactions commonly use hydrogen and its isotopes (deuterium and tritium), which fuse to release enormous energy.

A is incorrect: Uranium and thorium are used in nuclear fission, not fusion.

B is incorrect: Carbon and nitrogen feature in stellar CNO cycles but the common fusion fuel is hydrogen isotopes.

D is incorrect: Oxygen and helium are not the standard fusion fuels.

💡 The Sun is powered by the fusion of hydrogen nuclei into helium.
Q.No 22 Science & TechnologyHindi⚠ low OCR confidence
जब आप खड़े होने के बजाय रेत पर लेट जाते हैं, तो रेत पर दबाव ___ पड़ता है।
A
बड़ा
B
कम
✓ Correct
C
एक-रूप
D
शून्य

Solution

Correct answer: B. Lying down spreads your weight over a larger area, so the pressure on the sand is less (pressure = force / area).

A is incorrect: Pressure becomes smaller, not bigger, over a larger area.

C is incorrect: The pressure is reduced, not merely 'uniform'.

D is incorrect: The pressure decreases but is not zero.

💡 This is why lying flat helps distribute weight on soft or unstable surfaces.
Q.No 33 Science & TechnologyHindi
2025 में, भारतीय रेलवे ने में देश के पहले हाइड्रोजन-संचालित कोच का सफलतापूर्वक परीक्षण किया।
A
चेन्नई
✓ Correct
B
रायबरेली
C
कपूरथला
D
कोलकाता

Solution

Correct answer: A. In 2025 Indian Railways successfully tested the country's first hydrogen-powered coach at Chennai (Integral Coach Factory).

B is incorrect: Rae Bareli was not the test location cited.

C is incorrect: Kapurthala was not where it was tested.

D is incorrect: Kolkata was not the location.

💡 Hydrogen trains emit only water vapour, supporting green transport goals.
Q.No 21 Science & TechnologyEnglish
Water has maximum density at:
A
O°c
B
4°c
✓ Correct
C
25°C
D
100°C

Solution

Correct answer: B. Water has its maximum density at 4 degrees Celsius; below this it expands again, which is why ice floats.

A is incorrect: At 0 C water is freezing into ice, which is less dense.

C is incorrect: At 25 C water is less dense than at 4 C.

D is incorrect: At 100 C water is at its boiling point and far less dense.

💡 This 'anomalous expansion' of water lets aquatic life survive under ice in winter.
Q.No 31 Science & TechnologyEnglish
What is the gross weight of ISRO’s PSLV-XL Configuration?
A
120 tonnes
B
1040 tonnes
C
320 tonnes
✓ Correct
D
560 tonnes

Solution

Correct answer: C. ISRO's PSLV-XL configuration has a gross lift-off weight of about 320 tonnes.

A is incorrect: 120 tonnes is far too light.

B is incorrect: 1040 tonnes is far too heavy for the PSLV.

D is incorrect: 560 tonnes overstates the PSLV-XL's mass.

💡 The PSLV-XL uses larger strap-on boosters for heavier payloads, such as Chandrayaan-1 and Mangalyaan.
Q.No 32 Science & TechnologyEnglish
Which of the following discovery has been made with AstroSat? I. The detection of ultraviolet emissions emanating from remote galaxies. II. The polarisation of X-rays in proximate supernova remnants.
A
Only I
B
Only II
C
Both I and II
✓ Correct
D
Neither I nor II

Solution

Correct answer: C. AstroSat has contributed to both - detecting ultraviolet emissions from distant galaxies (I) and studying X-ray polarisation in supernova remnants (II) - so 'Both I and II' is correct.

A is incorrect: 'Only I' omits the X-ray polarisation work.

B is incorrect: 'Only II' omits the UV-emission discoveries.

D is incorrect: 'Neither' is wrong as both have been achieved.

💡 AstroSat (2015) is India's first dedicated multi-wavelength space observatory.
Q.No 33 Science & TechnologyEnglish
Which IIT is leading the development of the ‘Shakti’ microprocessor?
A
IIT Bombay
B
IIT Delhi
C
IIT Madras
✓ Correct
D
IIT Kanpur

Solution

Correct answer: C. IIT Madras is leading the development of the indigenous 'Shakti' microprocessor.

A is incorrect: IIT Bombay is not leading the Shakti project.

B is incorrect: IIT Delhi is not the lead.

D is incorrect: IIT Kanpur is not the lead.

💡 Shakti is based on the open-source RISC-V instruction set architecture.
Q.No 34 Science & TechnologyEnglish
In December 2024, which institute unveiled a cost-effective Bluetooth Low Energy Gateway with advanced data security to enhance IoT applications?
A
IIT Delhi
B
IIT Ropar
✓ Correct
C
IIT Madras
D
IIT Bombay

Solution

Correct answer: B. In December 2024 IIT Ropar unveiled a cost-effective Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) gateway with advanced data security for IoT applications.

A is incorrect: IIT Delhi was not credited with this.

C is incorrect: IIT Madras was not the developer cited.

D is incorrect: IIT Bombay was not the developer.

💡 BLE gateways link low-power IoT sensors to the internet/cloud.
Q.No 38 Science & TechnologyEnglish
What is the shape of the magnetic field lines around a current-carrying solenoid?
A
Random and irregular.
B
Parallel straight lines inside the solenoid and concentric circles outside.
✓ Correct
C
Concentric circles centered on the solenoid.
D
Radial lines extending outward from the solenoid.

Solution

Correct answer: B. The magnetic field inside a current-carrying solenoid is made of parallel, straight, uniform field lines, while outside it resembles a bar magnet (field lines looping around) - among the options, 'parallel straight lines inside, circles outside' best describes it.

A is incorrect: The field is not random and irregular; it is well-ordered.

C is incorrect: Pure concentric circles describe the field around a straight wire, not a solenoid.

D is incorrect: The field is not radial.

💡 A solenoid carrying current behaves like a bar magnet, the basis of electromagnets.
Q.No 40 Science & TechnologyEnglish
Which of the following is the use of petroleum gas in liquid form (LPG), a constituent of petroleum?
A
Ointments, candles, vaseline etc.
B
Fuel for heavy motor vehicles, electric generators
C
Paints, road surfacing
D
Fuel for home and industry
✓ Correct

Solution

Correct answer: D. Liquefied Petroleum Gas (LPG), a constituent of petroleum, is used as a fuel for homes (cooking) and industry.

A is incorrect: Ointments, candles and vaseline come from petroleum jelly/wax, not LPG.

B is incorrect: Heavy motor vehicles and generators mainly use diesel, not LPG.

C is incorrect: Paints and road surfacing relate to bitumen/other fractions, not LPG.

💡 LPG is mainly propane and butane, stored under pressure as a liquid.
Q.No 21 Science & TechnologyEnglish
0°୯ ରେ ବାୟୁରେ ଶବ୍ଦର ବେଗ କେତେ ?
A
344 m/s
B
331 m/s
✓ Correct
C
316 m/s
D
346 m/s

Solution

Correct answer: B. The speed of sound in air at 0 degrees Celsius is about 331 m/s.

A is incorrect: 344 m/s is the speed at about 20-22 C, not at 0 C.

C is incorrect: 316 m/s is too low for 0 C.

D is incorrect: 346 m/s corresponds to a warmer temperature.

💡 The speed of sound in air increases by about 0.6 m/s for each 1 C rise in temperature.
Q.No 33 Science & TechnologyHindi
को प्राय: प्रकृति के सफाई कर्मचारी के रूप में संदर्भित किया जाता है।
A
गिद्धों
✓ Correct
B
हाथियों
C
शेर
D
बाघ

Solution

Correct answer: A. Vultures are often called nature's scavengers (sanitation workers) because they feed on dead animal carcasses, helping keep the environment clean.

B is incorrect: Elephants are herbivores, not scavengers.

C is incorrect: Lions are predators, not scavengers in this sense.

D is incorrect: Tigers are predators, not nature's cleaners.

💡 India's vulture populations crashed due to the veterinary drug diclofenac, now banned for cattle.
Q.No 34 Science & TechnologyEnglish
निम्नलिखित में से किस वर्ष भारतीय राष्ट्रीय अंतरिक्ष अनुसंधान समिति का इसरो में विकास हुआ था?
A
1962
B
1969
✓ Correct
C
1972
D
1974

Solution

Correct answer: B. The Indian National Committee for Space Research (INCOSPAR) evolved into ISRO in 1969.

A is incorrect: 1962 is when INCOSPAR was set up, not ISRO.
